detail: We reported this problem back in Nov 23 and despite someone on three separate occasions,taking time to spray a bit of yellow paint on the kerb, unsurpisingly it hasnt fixed the problem. We had to have a front tyre replaced on our car due to bumping up and down on the loose kerb every time we enter the driveway. People use the drop in the kerb to cross the road near a very busy corner at the top of College Road and could easily lose their footing if rushing. This needs to be addressed soon.

title: Congestion
description: Congestion: Tail backs are forming when traffic, which has the right of way, approaches the speed 'hump' slows down to negotiate this 'traffic calming bump. Vehicles on the opposite side waiting to cross are backed up until all vehicles have crossed. Whilst this happens vehicles cannot pull out of Wilfred Own Road as there is no where for them to go as approximately 3 or 4 cars are in the queue . My concern is that when the new housing estate is open, the exit onto Middleton Road will will at times become grid locked. I counted 12 cars waiting to cross this 'hump' as I sat waiting to travel along Middleton Road to the Post Office. I don't use Cabin lane as I would have to drive over 7 traffic calming measures!! I trust that this issue was thought about when planning permission was originally granted?

detail:
I’ve noticed this street light has been off for ages now whilst the others are on. It used to go off at 12am understandably, when most are asleep. But it doesn’t even come on at all. I’m writing this 20 past 9 at night. But even now it’s getting dark it’s not on at all. I believe it’s faulty and not been checked. It’s quite dangerous since I live with my grandad and he comes back from going out in the evening around 9/10pm. So it’s a risk for him because he won’t be able to see aswell just outside near our home so I’m quite concerned. I’m his granddaughter. I live at 7 beech grove and it’s just outside the house this streetlight. It’s a nuisance for me especially if I need to pop outside or if I’m coming back from a friends one evening I can’t see at all since it’s pitch black now around the streetlight so it’s not safe. I’d be very grateful if you could sort this issue. I also feel safe at the evening with that street light on outside so I’m aware of passers by but without it makes me slightly anxious. Since I wouldn’t be able to see who’s coming to the door if approached.

Kind regards, Sian

Water coming up through the tarmac
description:
Water coming up through the tarmac: Constantly through this summer this part of the road is wet, causing a skid hazard on a brow of a hill and bend. Creating wet sounds even when it's sunny outside. Making verges sodden, especially side of the occupiers house and verges below are getting smelly and damaged.

Water company have been out to mend it after a blow hole a few metres happened however the remaining issues are that even being dug up 3 times in the last 18 mths no difference has been made for the highway users or the occupier.

Despite this issue has be acknowledged it continues.

description:
Traffic lights too confusing and too fast!: The traffic lights on Leg Street were changed recently. They switch far too quickly though, so this causes congestion on leg street, which is far busier than King Street, and people are pushing their luck to get through on a yellow, so speeding up to get through. The change to green on King Street seems to be a bit too quick to allow these thrill seekers through safely too.

And then, having two sets of traffic lights might be causing some confusion. I think the first car in this picture thought it was allowed up to the second set of lights. Or it might have just chickened out and stopped when it saw cars coming at it from the right. It was stopped there. Larger vehicles need that space to get round that corner.

If it hasn't happened already, this will cause an accident sooner or later. Please can someone come and have a look and a good think about it?

detail: A year ago I reported the Old Oswestry Hill Fort Information Board in Gatacre Park which is in a terrible condition and cannot be read due to the old broken perspex. It was apparently referred to an internal department but as yet nothing has been done and it continues to look an eyesore. Considering it is one of the best hill forts in Britain and many visitors to Oswestry come to see it, please could this site get the respect it deserves by installing a new board. Or if that isn't possible, to have it removed completely. Apologies if I have not contacted the relevant department but I would be grateful if this could be pursued. With kind regards.

At around 9.45pm on Saturday 31st August, my husband tripped on a raised paving slab when walking from St Oswalds Church to his car parked on Festival Square. The fall was very severe. Two young men came out of the Wynstay opposite and tried to lift him up. They couldn't get him up as he was in excruciating pain. So the men called 999, went to get blankets to make him more comfortable, and waited with him until the paramedics arrived. They assessed him and thought he'd broken his left hip. They managed to get him into the ambulance and took him to A&E at Royal Shrewsbury Hospital. He arrived at about 11pm and was assessed in the Emergency Centre, sent for an xray in the early hours of Sunday 1st September and operated on, a partial hip replacement, at around 3pm on the Sunday. He then spent 2 weeks in RSH followed by 12 days in the Orthopaedic Hospital on a rehab ward. He was discharged home on Wednesday 25th September with Shropshire Council's START Team visiting morning and evening to help with personal care. The Community Therapy Team are also involved with the OT providing various pieces of equipment to help, and the physio to help with exercises. Recovery is gong to be a long slow process.
title: Raised paving slab
description: Raised paving slab: At around 9.45pm on Saturday 31st August, my husband tripped on a raised paving slab when walking from St Oswalds Church to his car parked on Festival Square. The fall was very severe. Two young men came out of the Wynstay opposite and tried to lift him up. They couldn't get him up as he was in excruciating pain. So the men called 999, went to get blankets to make him more comfortable, and waited with him until the paramedics arrived. They assessed him and thought he'd broken his left hip. They managed to get him into the ambulance and took him to A&E at Royal Shrewsbury Hospital. He arrived at about 11pm and was assessed in the Emergency Centre, sent for an xray in the early hours of Sunday 1st September and operated on, a partial hip replacement, at around 3pm on the Sunday. He then spent 2 weeks in RSH followed by 12 days in the Orthopaedic Hospital on a rehab ward. He was discharged home on Wednesday 25th September with Shropshire Council's START Team visiting morning and evening to help with personal care. The Community Therapy Team are also involved with the OT providing various pieces of equipment to help, and the physio to help with exercises. Recovery is gong to be a long slow process.

title: Dog mess
description:
Dog mess: Hi street clean team, I've had reports of a lot of dog mess around the area of the one stop shop at the rear of dog mess. This is right next to the meadows school and needs attention ASAP. I would also ask that a warden is to monitor the area for the offenders please as it's not right that children have the risk of standing in it and having the embarrassment of having it on their shoes! Shropshire council now have a policy in place for this issue and I would be great full if this policy is used and action is taken to catch the people that are not clearing up after their dogs.

Kind regards cllr Les Maguire Cabin Lane ward.

description: Elderly person fall on blue painted road surface: The blue painted road surface is very difficult for some people to see and distinguish where the edge of the pavement is. My elderly father in law (aged 81) fell off this pavement recently and landed very heavily on his ribs and arms. This was on Saturday evening (4th October) at about 9pm. He was walking from the direction of Cross St and chatting to his brother. After passing the zebra crossing on his right, he approached the corner of pavement where the blue painted surface starts, he did not see that this was the edge of the pavement and fell very heavily and directly onto his front. He was very winded and took several minutes to recover before he as able to stand up and continue the walk home. He has been advised that he probably has a cracked rib but it is not worth an x-ray to confirm.

No 20mph sign on this end of school lane.: 20mph sign missing on school lane. One end of the lane has them, this end does not. So drivers entering from the Chirk Road side have no way of knowing there has been a speed reduction.

There is also a missing No cars accept for access signs.

I reported that there are no street lights on a large section of Whittington Road… they have now been out for months. This is hugely dangerous as it’s pitch black outside … unable to see pavement or people. We are opposite the Derwin College and there are vulnerable people living there and they must find it a nightmare walking to the local shop. We also have lots of elderly people living on the road. Last night, there was a man drunk in the street who needed help… I rang the police as he was nearly hit by a car but also completely lost. I was unable to go outside due to no street lightings and for my own safety. This matter remains urgent.

I'm not sure which dept to contact about this. This area of woodland and scrub owned by Shropshire Council,used to be owned by the then Oswestry Borough Council. Residents say it was well looked after in the past and cut regularly with a tractor. Since then the brambles and general scrub spread across and over the gardens of residents backing onto it and look very unsightly. An elderly resident in his 80's cuts it back to clear an area directly behind their gardens, his wife is very concerned about his safety continuing to do this. Can you let me know what arrangements are in place from Shropshire Council to look after this piece of their land, what cutting schedule is in place etc.

detail: The Loading Bay outside Weatherspoons, Willow Street Oswestry has had the Traffic Order sign removed off the Post and the 'Loading Bay' has been rubbed out on the tarmac. As a result private cars are able to use this space reserved for Loading only with then Delivery vehicles park in the Bus Stop. From the 30th of November 2024 Tanat Valley Coaches Town Service's with have a Stand time of 10min per trip, (buses arrive every 15min) If access to the bus stop is unavailable due to other vehicle's parking in the bus stop buses will be forced to wait there time in the Carriageway all buses are GPS linked so will be clocked as Running Early, which is then reported automatically to the Traffic Commissioner.

detail: There should be two parking spoaces on Morda Road outside Cae Hendre and Cromer House. Taking these away is not in accordance with the Road Traffic Act for this area. When it was first mooted that there shoukd be lines installed, it was decided against by the relevant committee. No further decision has been made. Traffic parked there helps to create a chicane effect which slows traffic before it reaches the sevondary school with 2000 pupils. When the ropad was resurfaced the lines were replaced wrongly and have now been extended. Either take the lines out or make Edward Street a controlled parking zone for residents.

The problem is not with the breaking up of tarmac but the extremely uneven surface. I'm not sure how well it would photograph. The road surface is so ridiculously uneven & so makes any drive up and down Monkmoor Road extremely bumpy. We live just of this road. There is no other way of us exiting from our home address other than to travel on that road. It's been a nightmare for years and years. We've been waiting and hoping that it would eventually come up on the council's list of repairs. I have worked for Oswestry Borough & Shropshire Council, and also a Parish Council, & have also worked for a road surfacing company, so have some experience of the pressures that are put on councils/councillors and funds etc. We have been trying to avoid adding to that pressure ourselves for years & years. It won't have done our cars any good in the many years we've lived here. But now I have poor health so also have to have my mobility scooter in the back of the car very often. I need this to give me some form of independence. But the scooter is also now getting battered about, as well as the car itself. We have kept hoping it would have come up naturally on the schedule of works ages ago. Other residents must be suffering, too. We are at the point now of thinking we must try to finally to get something done about it. We have thought of prepping a petition type letter and getting everyone to sign it etc...There are very many residents who will have to use this road. It is unavoidable for so many. But decided to contact the council first directly. We are hoping that the council will see (easily) how bad it is & understand how patient we have been & finally get instruction to do some work to level it out. I'd like the council to consider if it were their only access-road when leaving their home how they would feel.

title: Drains / road gulleys blocked
description: Drains / road gulleys blocked: Following recent surface flooding in Gobowen the remaining water is now sitting on the road surface rather than draining away despite there being no water adding to it. The water level hasn't changed for 3 hours. This is a clear indication that the gulleys are blocked. In previous flooding events the water drained clearly from drains in the side streets but not from the drains on the main through road, despite them connecting and going through the same downstream path - a further indication there is a blockage problem. It appears to be blocked between the terrace houses on Station Rd and the railway line.

description: Sunken kerbs and no drainage for surface water run-off.: When we have a period of rain a pool of rainwater builds up at the entrance to no.7 Whitefriars and then floods onto the driveway and flows towards the building foundations. This has caused the driveway to crack and block paving to sink. The problem is the road kerbs have sunk allowing the water to flow over the footpath and onto the driveway. The kerbs need to be raised and maybe an additional gulley installed. Apart from the damage caused to the driveway it is a hazard when the water freezes on the footpath which is used frequently by the public, also the tarmac on the footpath needs resurfacing as it is degrading. Thank you

detail: There is a black 3 series and a blue 3 series that are constantly parked directly on the corner of the junction. The black bmw 3 series has not moved in many months. It is illegal to park so close to give way sign and junction. It is constantly causing an issue when vehicles try to turn up to Gitten street, have to reverse back into oncoming traffic on a main road, this is again illegal and very very dangerous. There needs something putting in place urgently before an accident happens. Gitten street should either be a 1 way road, or you should place permanent bollards to prevent people parking within 10 meters of a give way sign, this would leave sufficient space for the turning vehicle to pull into.

title: Bad road surface and pot holes
description:
Bad road surface and pot holes: Regarding the appalling state of the end of back racecourse lane (trefonen end) . I'm not sure whether to describe them as potholes or craters to be honest. I'm sure I saw a fiat 500 in one of them 🤭. I personally think the waterworks should bare the cost of repairs to that part of the road as it's obviously due to all the heavy traffic ( cement mixers/ tipper lorries ect) going there. The road is in much better condition after the turning for the site traffic. Many thanks

This is a message sent to me from a concerned resident that's asked for my help in the situation..

Can you please inform me if work is due to be done, if so when?

If not can this work be carried out asap.

Any update or information on this would be appreciated.
detail:

description: Flooding due to lack of maintenance of waterways: Every time we get heavy rain, the weir by the side of the road opposite School House diverts the water onto the road. This is because it is not maintained or routinely cleared of accumulated debris. The same comment applies to several of the roadside drains further downstream. The result is that the road outside our property becomes a raging torrent, as the photo shows, and this has happened three times in the last two months. The force and depth of the water stops all traffic and is so strong that it is dangerous to even attempt to walk in it, cutting several houses off. Now the road surface is being broken up by the force of the water, with large suspension-damaging potholes developing. Although our property is just high enough to avoid being flooded by this torrent, the same cannot be said of some living further down.

title: Holbache rd residents illegally blocking the road with cones to reserve parking spaces.
detail: I’ve been reporting this issue to you since April and have spoken to you by phone. Seemingly no action taken to date. Residents are using cones to block parking spaces on Holbache Rd. Oswestry. We all want to park by our houses but you can’t if someone else has parked there. So park elsewhere. Some residents are placing cones outside their home to reserve parking spaces outside their homes. It illegal according to the Highway Act and unfair on other road users. Recently a district nurse visiting a disabled resident could not park due to the cone warriors and an ambulance attending an Emergancy- cones blocked parking access. While I understand residents want to park, it’s not ok to block parking access. There aren’t any parking restrictions or double yellow lines or resident only parking arrangements. Since reporting this issue in April and May, it’s become a bit of a contagion as more residents are now coning. One day recently nearly all of one side of the street was coned off by residents, some using “Police” cones nicked following the recent balloon festival. A polite letter to residents may assist with their comprehension of this illegal and unfair activity. So, Shropshire Council, what are you going to do about it ?

4” deep x 30” x 12” potholes under water with the rain and not visible until you hit it.: Potholes are getting bigger and deeper and the rain makes them invisible until they are hit. I hit it last week in the bad weather and had to stop to see if there was any damage to my wheels. I also stopped on another occasion to offer help to a motorcyclist who had come off after hitting one of them. He wasn’t badly hurt but was soaking wet and his bike was damaged a bit. I think he’ll have to buy a new front wheel at the least. I was able to inflate his tire but don’t know if it stayed up for him. Other people have hit them hard all over the Christmas period.

B5009 floods regularly. Accidents happen.: A short section of the road floods quite deeply regularly. Several vehicles aqua plane. In November a car planed off the road in the dark and rolled into the hedge taking out the hedge. The field had livestock which had they escaped could cause further accidents. Fortunately I was able to contact the farmer who although not living near was able to make a speedy repair. The small boy passenger and driver were ok. I fear More accidents will likely occur if a solution isn’t found. The issue has been there since I moved here in February 2021.Flood signs are sometimes put up but then left long after the water has gone and so the signs are no longer effective.

This is an extremely busy road used to get into town by cars and pedestrians.

The surgery is used by elderly residents and when it's slippery it's a hazard.

Please can we have it back ASAP.

Cllr Les Maguire Cabin Lane ward.

title: Street light out
description:
Street light out: Street light number 4 has been out for over a year!!! This light is right next to a zebra crossing which is used by school children!! I've whitened cars not stopping to let pedestrians cross possibly due to the fact you can't see them as this light is out!! If this is the case then God forbid this is to happen when a child is crossing and is knocked down as a result of bad lighting!

I'm also forwarding this on to the portfolio holder of highways to flag this as a dangerous situation. Please can this be given priority attention before someone is killed. As you can see from the picture taken is very dark in this area..

Kind regards cllr Les Maguire

title: Massive sink hole forming in the road
description: Massive sink hole forming in the road: This started as a pothole over a year ago. Now it’s turning in to a sink hole. Why is nobody fixing this issue. We were fobbed off back in July with they are looking to fix ASAP. We were now in mid January 2025. This needs to be sorted ASAP. There are also pot hole at the junction too. Plus down near D Powell on lower Brooke street is falling apart, literally the whole of the road and also Victoria Street has a massive pot hole. How much longer are we going to fobbed off before these issues are fixed. There are going to be lots of damaged cars at the councils fault. What do we pay road tax and council tax for? All you seem to do is put flowers around the town.

title: Lack of appropriate and missing signage
description:
Lack of appropriate and missing signage: It's been many months since my initial report, and I've not received any updates, so I wanted to highlight a few ongoing issues following a site visit.

🔸️Chirk Road needs to be 20MPH during school hours. Many families need to use Chirk Road to drop off and collect their children. As a result, a large amount of pupils are exiting school and needing to cross the road. Often between cars, as the road becomes very busy. There are no VAS signs despite funding being in place, no lighted signage to warn drivers they are entering a school zone, no proper crossing and no speed reducing measures in place. There really are a lot of options available to make the area safer for pupils and parents alike.

School lane is now missing it's 20MPH signage following my report of mismatched signage on either end of the lane. As a result, drivers are now unaware of the speed limit change and are driving down School Lane assuming it is 30MPH in line with the joining roads.

After standing on the junction I need to highlight that urgent action is required to bring the roads in line with the safety standards of other local schools. Please correspond at your earliest convenience.

Many thanks Cllr Jay Moore

Council Land Stolen and footpath obstructed
detail:
The owner of "Avalon" has quite clearly stolen a huge chunk of council land over recent years. This has been reported numerous times, and even when emailing the council, they say to report it on "fix my street", and nothing is ever done. He has reduced visibility onto the main road causing danger to local residents and his regular building waste dumped on the pavement forces children to walk in the road to pass. Evidence is quite clearly seen in google street-view history, and I have even attached the land registry proving the land he has stolen.

Please advise on where this needs to be reported to rather than just ignoring it as usual.

detail: Just outside Oswyn House Dental Practice, 22/22 OswaldRoad, there used to be a disabled bay. When the road was resurfaced recently the disabled writing on the road was not reinstated. Please can this happen because it is very very difficult to stop to let out a disabled person (eg.my husband) who needs a walker to walk, to get into the dentist. The entrance is on the side which is very narrow road access to the car park area. I cannot stop outside the entrance as the road is too narrow to open the car doors. And I need to be able to get the walker out from behind the drivers seat. If I park in the car park its too far for him to safely walk even with the walker. If I can park in the parking bay on Oswald Road he can just manage to get in, and I can safely get the walker out. He has a blue badge.

service_code: Tree problem on an open space
description:
Why has this tree been cut down: I would like to know why a sycamore tree has been cut down adjacent to my property on watts dyke, Tuesday 11th February 2025?

The damage the tractor has done to the soft ground is awful, how is this to be rectified? Please see attached photograph.

It is totally bizzaire as to why this has been done. I certainly didn't request this and did not want the tree cut down. Why this tree and not the other 5? Please see attached photograph.

Also, as anybody with half a brain will know, just cutting a sycamore down to approx 2ft is pointless. Please see attached photograph. This will encorage vigorous new growth.

Surely as Shropshire council is strapped for cash, this has been a needless waste of money.

I do not wish for any trees adjacent to my property being cut down. I actually maintain them myself as the help to support my fence.

Answers please.

Michael Wilson 12 Border Close, Oswestry, Shropshire, SY112TT

‪07875544814‬ Mw92829@gmail.com

Over grown hedge row on road side of council recycling centre: The hedge on the road side of the oswestry recycling centre hasn't been cut back for quite a few years. Iv reported this problem before and have recently been told its not an issue. I suggest that the a member of the the shropshire council should take a ride out in a larger vehicle to see how the difficult it is passing 2 large vehicles on this stretch of road. As you can see in the pictures uploaded that the hedge on the property opposite has been cut back regularly and not encouraging into the road. I hope that the shropshire council review their decision on this stretch of road before members of the public, local farmers or businesses have major damage caused to their vehicles whilst passing here.

The drain cover (culvert?) directly behind the kerbstone has been removed and just left on the grass verge. It was too heavy for me to lift it, but it is right on the edge where a car can drive over it.

I would say it has been removed within the last 7 days (I walk the lane regularly and don't remember seeing it last week).

description:
Parking on public footpath: Residents and visitors to Erw Wen are parking on pathways blocking more and more of the pathways in Erw Wen. The issue starts when lower belle Vue meets Erw Wen.

Down the hill vehicles park on the left hand side not on pathways however cars have now started parking on the right hand side on the pathway blocking the pathway and making it difficult drive down the hill with cars parked either side of the road.

Cars block the entire path way if Erw Wen with residents parking between drive ways on patches of pathway too small for the vehicle making access on and off drives difficult.

Many school children, residents with dogs and buggy’s are being forced to walk in the road as they cannot access the pathways.

I have seen this issue reported as a blocked drain the photographs clearly show this is NOT the problem, as the drain cover can clearly be seen and is nto where the water is pooling and is not removing the standing water. The pooling is right in front of the pedestrian crossing, meaning when a car comes past they get soaked, when a car hits the puddle. This happens more often than you would think especially when two cars pass at the same time, with the traffic lights less than 100 yards away it is common for cars to come up the one way system two abreast and there is nowhere for the nearside car to go to miss the puddling. This is resulting in people preferring to take their chances moving further up the road and and not using the pedestrian crossing.

Mattresses, furniture and more has been dumped over the fence onto the grass. Unfortunately this waste is right up against a residents property and poses a potential fire risk. The grass bank is owned by Shropshire Council and needs a fence erecting in order to stop this repeatedly occurring.

Many thanks, Cllr Jay Moore

description:
2 fallen trees. One is dangerous: While in Gobowen I came across these 2 fallen trees. One is on the ground on the green, but a second is actually laying across a residents fence and over their property boundary.

When speaking with neighbours, I learned that this tree has been in situe since storm Daragh and this tree has been left over their property, despite numerous reports since December. I'm honestly at a loss as to how it could have been left there for so long as it clearly needs immediate attention before it causes further damage, or worse, injury.

Many thanks for your help, Cllr Jay Moore

It's been an issue for a number of years

Also the big tree that fell on the other side of the park has been cut back leaving just a trunk laying on the floor. Again it looks a mess. Could this be turned into a nice seating area by it being carved out? Rather than just leaving it ? Be nice to make something from it rather than chopping it up or just leaving it as it is...

detail:
I'm sure you are more than aware of the situation regarding flytipping at Crestwood Court, thanks to the sheer quantity of reports stretching back to 2023.

Since then, we have: Had several areas cleared. Funded and installed CCTV. Had the area confirmed as the worst for flytipping in Oswestry by Veolia. Liased with West Mercia Police, STAR Housing, Shropshire Fire Prevention team, residents, neighbours and more.

Residents are understandably furious with the situation, and it seems to just be delay after delay.

This area needs clearing immediately. It is now dry, and is a real fire hazard. I am struggling to find any word at this point other than neglect.

The Town Council is now spending money to try and tackle the issue. It is time Shropshire Council clear it, so residents can enjoy some time outside as the weather improves without having to live next to the rotting food waste and eye sore of dozens of mattresses and white goods.

Please clear it. It really needs putting to the top of the schedule now. It's been far too long and residents in the area have suffered for too long.

Kind regards, Cllr Jay Moore

Could advice be given to put the bollards away from the direction of the path before someone is seriously injured here?

description:
Dangerous potholes near to care home.: I've had reports of few dangerous pot holes just outside the Old Vicarage Care Home along the walkway towards the village in Gobowen.

A few weeks ago a Gentleman in his 80s has tripped and fell on one of these holes which has resulted in a nasty head injury that required stitches. Both potholes are very deep and given the location and proximity to a care home and the resulting injuries already reported, I feel this really needs to be prioritised to avoid further incident.

Whilst there I spoke with several staff members. One noted that wheelchair users need to go in the road due to the angle of the dropped curb in order to access the pavement. It really isn't suitable.

Many thanks, Cllr Jay Moore

detail: The pavement in front of our house has started to sag and dip, and a sinkhole has started to form in the tarmac by our gate. The drains in the road were flooding lots at christmas and I reported them (you sent the gully sucker out in january). However since then the pavement has sagged and a sinkhole has appeared. I visited the council archives to try and figure out if any services pass under this section, and the road drains drain to a soakaway under our driveway apparently. My understanding is that despite the soakaway being within our land, its the council's responsibility to maintain it. I am now concerned that the soakaway isn't functioning properly, or the soakaway pipe may be blocked and this is having a knock-on effect for the stability of the surrounding area. The tarmac at the join for the pavement to our boundary wall is starting to crack too and I don't want there to be any issues with our driveway access or indeed the foundations of our home or stability of the wall, as the soakaway is from the 1960's and is located less than 5m from our house which isn't acceptable by modern standards. Please could someone investigate?

Spraying weedkiller without notifying residents
detail:
Weedkiller is regularly sprayed along the roads, and pathways on the playing fields, where residents walk their dogs and children play- there is no notification that this has/will occur, allowing children,pets and residents to be exposed to the sprays.

The verge along Fernhill Avenue is teeming with wildflowers and pollinators but regularly sprayed. Please could the Council refrain from spraying weedkiller around my home and neighbourhood? There is no acceptable reason to spray weedkiller now that we are all aware of the devastating crash in insect and wild bird populations.

Road work barriers not removed: Work on the charging station in the Highwayman Pub has involved digging up Greystones Way. Residents have endured 5 weeks of dust, noise and disturbance and restricted access. Now all the barriers, cones, sandbags have been abandoned over the entrance to Greystones Way. This is despite there being a compound within the Highwayman car park where all this could have been secured. This would have prevented local vandals scattering the barriers about during the night. After 5 weeks of disturbance we now have an eyesore. Please arrange for this to be sorted out now.

description:
More fly-tipping: As we know. Leaving flytipped waste in an area for extended periods of time attracts more flytipped waste. The affected area is now spreading due to a continued lack of action by Shropshire Council.

The area needs clearing properly, and the fence rebuilt that the top of the hill to discourage this behaviour. Residents have been dealing with this situation for years. It it costing more money in the long run and neglecting tax paying residents. We really need thus addressing.

Blocked toilet drainage at BUTTERFLY Dental Practice, Victoria Road: We have a blocked drain on the Salop Road side between the wall of our premises (BUTTERFLY Dental Practice) and the hedge. We have checked that our pipes are clear and the sewage is flowing freely into our drain, but it is not being moved any further towards Avalon because something is blocking it on the way. It seems that one of the drains belonging to Avalon is blocked - we do not have access to them. Please intervene quickly as we are at risk of being literally flooded with faeces, which will force us to temporarily suspend dental services.

Can someone tell me who owns the carpark that is accessed from Hawthorn grove in Oswestry please? Residents of lime grove and college road have access from the rear of their properties, someone has dug out the ground to park their car, there is already a trailer and jetski against the fence, now there are loads of pallets and a disassembled shed there. Where the ground has been dug out has left a wall of hard soil that's about 18 inches high and I need to get to the back of my fence so that I can cut back the brambles and a fir hedge to stop them pushing through the wooden panels, I have balance problems so I can't get up and over the soil bank to maintain the rear of my fence. Also I would like to point out that the residents let their children play on the carpark and the constant screaming and shouting from 3.30pm till 8pm school days and all day on the weekends is driving my stress levels through the roof which is preventing me from wanting to spend any time in my garden. Not being able to drive and being on a low income means my garden is the only place I can get any real enjoyment. Hopefully a resolution can be found for both issues, thank you.

detail: Refered to you from Oswestry then Shropshire Councils. A "virtual" road crossing has been created on Cabin Lane between Unicorn Park and Broadlands way park. I note sadly it took a serious injury to a child for a zebra crossing to be installed in Unicorn road. A pro-active approach to Cabin Lane may prevent injury, serious injury or even a death. Thank you

title: Overgrown tree
description: Overgrown tree: The tree has over grown and is dropping tree sap onto our car, this is causing us an increase in cost due to the increase in water charges as we are on a water meter and we are having to wash our car daily. We are concerned this Sap is going to damage the paint work. It is sprayed all over the car and has thick droplets left that do not come off easily. We can not park on our drive and have to park on the grass. (Picture) this is something we should not have to do. The tree roots are also growing through to our pipes we have removed and cleared the roots from inside the man whole. This is going to cause serious damage to ours and our neighbours pipes if not treated. Can this tree be removed? Thank you for your support in this matter.

The end of the pipe as it enters the brook is damaged. We have excavated with a mini digger and have found it needs a 3.5 metre piece of glaced pipe to fit onto the pipe to take the water into the brook.
title: The drain at Albridge Lane Morton Oswestry has been cleaned out, but the end of the pipe which goes from under the railway bridge through our field into the brook has been damaged. We have excavated with a mini digger to uncover the pipe. A new 3.5 metre pipe is reqiured to fit on the damaged end.

detail: The are 3 parking bays for disabled outside of the old Shropshire CC building, next to the library. I went back to the shops to give the illegally parked car time to move and on returning found that when he had left ANOTHER Car pulled into the same gap, again illegally parked and I had to make him move so I could get into my car. This somewhat of an urgent matter as there are very few disabled places in Oswestry Town Centre and illegally parked vehicles are making it hard for genuine disabled users.

Pulling out of Mount Drive is increasingly becoming an "accident waiting to happen". Cars parked along Mount Road (Mount Drive side) close to the junction of Miunt Drive, towards the direction of the racecourse are blocking any view of vehicles coming down Mount Road from the racecourse direction (as you can see from the picture), these vehicles do not tend to comply with the speed limit. In the opposite direction (heading into town) vehicles are again parked along Mount Road close to the juntion with Mount Drive, with limited visibility. One car in particular hasn't moved for over 6 months. Visibility is slightly better as you are "looking down" the road whereas towards the racecourse direction you are looking up the road and any car parked on that side restricts visibility. Coming out of Mount Drive you have to pull across one lane before being able to see if there on any oncoming vehicles coming down from the race course direction. This morning, when these photos were taken, I had to pull straight out of the drive and "hope for the best" as I couldn't see up the road due to the vehicles parked all the way up Mount Road or around the vehicle closest to the junction of Mount Drive. It is worse if the vehicle is a higher vehicle ie van. The vehicles tend to be people walking their dogs on the field next to the fire station or workers/visitors going into town. The road either needs double yellow lines along it or at least a mirror opposite Mount Drive so we can see vehicles coming.
title: Cars parked close to entrance and along street with little to no visability to pull out of junction. RTC hazard

There is a road crossing between ALDI and the bus station where cars can drive into ALDI. A couple of months ago I was hit by a van and went to hospital with a fractured foot. At the time I said that the driver of the van was not at fault because I am new to the area and did not realise cars enter that area. I assumed because I saw cars going to my right as I was crossing the road, that a car could not enter the road near me and only exit. But since this occasion, even when looking and attempting to cross the road on multiple occasions, I am finding myself having to step out in the middle of the road to see if cars are coming. It's not possible to see if cars are coming without doing stepping into the road and the bush to left is too over grown to see over it. It is dangerous and it does need changing.

title: No visibility when pulling out of Mount Drive
description: No visibility when pulling out of Mount Drive: Cars parked along Mount Road, close to the junction to Mount Drive mean that pulling out of Mount Drive is extremely dangerous. You literally cannot see a thing and have to just pull out and hope no one is coming along Mount Drive. This is made even worse by the fact that just up from this junction, the speed limit goes from 60 to 30, meaning that most people are also still speeding when then pass the junction. All we need that would resolve this issue are two mirrors on the lamppost opposite the junction to give visibility in each direction. This matter is seriously urgent. The issue has been reported in the past but nothing was done. Me and my 9 month old baby were almost in a crash yesterday and it’s only a matter of time before someone crashes here. The matter has become urgent as of last week because there is building work being done on a house close by and there are a lot more vehicles parked on Mount Drive than there normally are. This building work is going to be going on for at least 6 months so the issue really needs addressing immediately before someone is hurt.

Serious risk from badly degraded footpath surface
description:
Serious risk from badly degraded footpath surface: The surfaced path has now deteriorated to an extremely unsafe state. This pathway MUST be now be repaired appropriately as a matter of urgency. Large sections of tarmac have broken away; numerous potholes are deep and very unsafe. Many residents on the Agnes Hunt Memorial Bungalows are wheelchair users with mobility difficulties and are unable to use the footpath as a result. The situation has now become extremely hazardous for the many vulnerable residents living here.

Under the Equality Act 2010, public authorities, including local councils, have a duty to ensure that public spaces and highways are accessible to all individuals, including those with disabilities. The current state of Agnes Hunt Memorial Bungalows footpath surfacing fails to meet these standards, effectively preventing wheelchair users from accessing essential services, local businesses, and community spaces and leaving vulnerable residents isolated as a result.

I kindly request that Shropshire Council takes immediate action to repair the potholes and all dropped kerbs, to ensure that the footpath meets necessary accessibility requirements. Please provide a response outlining the steps that will be taken and a timeline for when repairs will be carried out.

Should this issue remain unresolved, I will have no choice but to escalate the matter to the Local Government Ombudsman and the various Disability Rights organisations for further review.

I do appreciate your prompt attention to this matter and look forward to your response. Please feel free to contact me should you require further information. Many thanks

detail:
Grass cutting of open space at parking area at top on Friars Street.

Myself and other residents are absolutely disgusted that The Council has allowed the disgraceful grass cutting to have taken place on the grass area at the top of Friars Street adjacent to the residents parking area. Like everything else in this town The Council has an extremely lax approach, bordering on negligence. The contractor should never have been paid for such a haphazard job. None of the cuttings have been cleared nor have the edges been trimmed. The residents of Friars Street as Council Tax payers would welcome the Council's views particularly from the person responsible for signing off on the task. I look forward to hearing from you.

description:
Hedges along road overgrown: Good morning "Brynhyfryd opposite Silverdale Drive Hedge protruding into highway from here up to their drive making it difficult for larger vehicles to pass without scratching the side or scuffing the wheels on the opposite curb trying to avoid the hedge. This was reported twice last year and nothing has happened. The hedge needs cutting back to their boundary wall.

Bryn Celyn (think this is owned by the house next door Just below) The corner of their property on the junction with Spring Bank. Again, the hedge bulges out as it goes up. There is a small layby type parking area opposite. If there is nothing parked there you can go onto this and pass ok but more often than not their are parked cars there which makes passing the hedge very tight.

Both properties need to cut the hedges straight up from their boundaries and it would put an end to the problems."

title: Oswestry wall and tree responsibility
description: Oswestry wall and tree responsibility: Hi, Whilst dealing with some casework in Laburnum Drive, Oswestry, I noted the poor and dangerous state of a section of the stone wall opposite 65 Laburnum Drive. It appears that trees have been allowed to grow out of the base of this wall, causing it to collapse. Since children play around this area (I did many years ago!), I am concerned for their safety should they attempt to climb around this gap. Who in the council should I approach about this. Thank you. Councillor Owen

detail:
We contacted Shropshire Council, with our concern of narrowing of pedestrian pavements about a year ago, particularly along a stretch of pavement (SY11 2HU) opposite Lidl Supermarket. Workmen attended to it fairly quickly, by just 'nipping' the ends of the sprawling greenery, which, of course, immediately encouraged further growth.

We write again, attaching 2 photos:- Victoria Road, opposite Lidl Supermarket, SY11 2HU, where the pavement is about 18" wide. No children's buggies or electric scooters are able to reach the traffic light system, enabling access to the supermarket, cemetery or the council estate further down Salop/Shrewsbury Road, used by school children, all of whom have to step out into the flow of traffic, coming from behind.

Salop Road, opposite Halfords, SY11 2RL, where a section of the pavement is also narrow, restricting access for people with buggies or electric scooters.

We had suggested that the owners of the these particular properties should be approached with a view to maintaining their hedges; but it seems as though the owner may actually be Shropshire County Council, who owns the triangular plot of land between Victoria Road and Salop Road.

Perhaps a robust 'hacking back' is what is required, to minimise repeated maintenance.

Kind regards,

Melvin and Carole Gough

description: Excessive Speed: The stretch of road between Kwik Fit and the roundabout connecting to the bypass is frequently used by motorists in an anti-social manner. Excessive speed is a common problem and also loud booming exhausts. As it is a straight stretch of road, motorists use it like it is a drag strip in both directions. It is most notable at night and we have been woken up several times by people driving this way. Would it be possible to investigate adding a speed camera to deter this anti-social behaviour?

detail:
There is a tree on the right hand side of the central path that is being held by dual stakes and some rope. The rope seems to have been there for years and has left some severe girdling, and it appears it has become damaged over time and the supports have rotted, and the tree is swaying significantly, I think because it seems to not be rooted very well.

It's current support is a very temporary measure and there seems to be a high risk that it will fall.

The section of pavement before The Mill in Morda, and after the New Drill Cottages, on Morda Bank has become dangerously narrow. This is due to the grass verge encroaching onto the pavement by at least two feet. For example, the bottom of a lamp post has now been swallowed up by the soil and grass. The narrow pavement only just fits the width of a pushchair, which is very hazardous as it is a commonly used path towards a primary school and preschool site. I believe it has been like this for many years, slowly getting worse over the last 10 years. The parallel pavement is also very narrow so cannot be used as an alternative, this pavement has always been narrow as it runs along a wall.

Blocked Drain, Beatrice Street, Oswestry
description:
Blocked Drain, Beatrice Street, Oswestry: There is a blocked drain in Beatrice Street, adjacent to The Railway Tavern public house and at the bottom of the junction with Swan Lane. It has been blocked for a considerable number of years which causes the road to flood during torrential rain. It's been reported dozens of times by residents including myself and at one stage, Shropshire Council did come out to dig it up, check it and fill it back in again, and that's all that has happened. The matter has not been resolved and still requires urgent attention.

A copy of this will be sent to the relevant Portfolio Holder.

title: Road Markings Missing
description: Road Markings Missing: I'm reporting on behalf of RAF Cadets 1165 (Oswestry) Squadron. Unfortunately there have been several recent incidents with vehicles parking outside the Drill Hall, completely blocking our gates. As a result we've been unable to exit or access the Drill Hall with our squadron mini bus, causing a great deal of inconvenience. As can be seen in the attached photos, the road markings outside our gates have faded to the point of non-existence, and we'd really appreciate if it could be arranged for them to be repainted as a priority. We are also looking to purchase some signs for the gates. Thanks very much!

description: Uneven footpath, hazard for wheelchair user & pedestrians.: Today I was coming along English Walls, Oswestry and had to squeeze past a lady who was trying to cross the road. My wheelchair suddenly careered sideways due to a significant dip in the pavement. I was very luck not to have pushed the lady into the road, into on coming traffic. The dip is in the pavement on the Car Park side of the road, about opposite Heron Foods shop. We were both a little shocked by what happened, but luckily I was able to avoid causing her injury - my wheelchair weighs a lot and could have caused a serious accident because the DIP in the pavement threw my wheels side weighs meaning I temporarily lost control. I consider repairs to this pavement to be URGENT.
title: Uneven footpath, hazard for wheelchair user & pedestrians.

You need to wear boots to walk along the pavement. An additional drain is needed urgently with a continuation of the gulley to send the water towards it.

description: Traffic calming humps: Out of the 12 speed bumps. 1 is missing, 9 have broken their fixings and are moving across the road leaving broken bolts sticking up, 2 are still in place. Have you considered measuring the average width of a van and making the gap between the speed bumps wider, this would encourage people to wait and drive between the bumps turning the road effectively into a single lane which is safer for the school children. The solid type of traffic calming used here was designed for use in schools, hospitals, car parks etc where speed is irrelevant. They are not designed for busier roads. Who ever ordered these didn’t check their use before installing them. If they were positioned as suggested people would drive between them as they do by the shop and wait patiently for the road to clear it’s a chance for Oswestry to make a difference to road safety in the area and get publicity for being innovative.

detail:
I've had the following reported to me, can you please take action to the following issues.

Good afternoon, Les not sure if you can help by knowing or contacting the department that look after the dog park the grass is still extremely long and sometimes when they come they’re not doing it properly they’re missing bits out and with all the leaves falling you can’t see any dog mess and it’s disgusting I stepped in some last week. Thought I’d give it the benefit of the doubt and see whether it gets cut Friday. It still hasn’t been cut. A week later almost I know they don’t cut it over the winter so hopefully there’s still at least one more cut left to do.

If this is done and the leaves are cleared this may make it easier for people to clean up after their dogs.

Also can I ask that the edges are strimmed please and leaves are cleared up.

Also Jennings rd and brynhaford rd are both covered in leaves which is now turning into mud which will soon slow the flow of water run off to drains and will also block the drains. Can this issue also be cleaned up simply by someone leaf blowing and s road sweeper picking it up.

Kind regards Les Maguire.

title: Inadequate signage and cone placement and raised curb with non compliant sign
description: Inadequate signage and cone placement and raised curb with non compliant sign: At approximately 0415 this morning i was travelling out of Oswestry on the B4579 going past an area of development, as I looked right prior to entering the roundabout I heard a loud bang as my wheel hit a pedestrian crossing feature that juts out into the highway bursting my tyre and damaging my steering, the area has new street lights but we're unlit and the bollard to demarcate this is non-conforming to NRSWA, in the wet and dark the base of this pedestrian feature blends into the highway, if this feature has yet to be commissioned the cones that are currently there should guide you around it not into it, at a time when I can barely afford to put food on the table I now have to get my car fixed, I suggest that the TM at this location is inadequate, I imagine i'm not the first person to be effected by this new crossing and likely won't be the last unless something is changed. The angle at which the crossing sits in the road is poor and the curb is barely visible, the bollard has no illumination and no blue directional arrow to highlight the edge of the crossing, please look into this. Once I find out what damage has been done to my steering I will be forced to take legal action to cover the expense of any repairs required to my vehicle.

Overgrown Leylandi trees
detail:
We have moved into 27 Rose Hill Drive, Whittington, Oswestry. SY11 4BE this week.

Directly behind our rear garden we have a number of leylandi trees which are 15 - 20 feet tall.

They appear to have grown unrestricted and are blocking the natural light. They are part of the landscaping for the properties behind our own and not in another properties garden.

I would be grateful if you could advise what the process is / who would I need to contact to get them pruned down.

Thank you for your help.

title: Faded road / lane markings making junction dangerous
detail:
The road markings are barely visible on the junction. As you approach , the left lane is to turn left into Oswestry town centre. The right lane is for turning right and for going straight on into Coppice Drive. Because of the faded arrows, vehicles are also going in the left lane to go straight on causing many near misses, as well as angry drivers, when 2 vehicles are simultaneously trying to go straight over.

I raised this point last year and in February you replied stating "New updates on Worn out road markings We have looked into this issue and can advise that the work required will be completed in our programme of upcoming routine maintenance.

State changed to: Closed"

No work has yet been carried out.

description: A broken pipe causing flooding onto the road, with a lot of water wastage, near Weston Lodge on Weston Lane.: This flooding water has occurred for about 5 months. There have been multiple reports of this I believe by many local residents to the authorities. I myself have contacted Severn Trent and United Utilities, who said they were not responsible for it. I learn now that it is probably the Highway agency who will look after this. With temperatures dropping I am very concerned that there will be a car/pedestrian accident due to ice. This broken pipe and the resulting water is on a fairly steep gradient, a bend and at a point where the road is single carriageway. Even my 22 year old son is very concerned about this. Please repair it soon before all the real bad weather.

detail:
There is a pedestrian light controlled crossing enabling pedestrians to cross the one way traffic from Beatrice Street continuing straight (onto Thomas Savin Road) or turning right (towards Oswald Road).

When the vehicle traffic is sat at the red light (enabling other traffic flows), it is safe for pedestrians to cross here. However throughout the red light phase here, the pedestrian indication continues to show red.

When the pedestristrian request button is pushed, pedestrians must wait for an entire sequence of traffic light changes in order to cross. Sometimes this means waiting 1 or 2 minutes longer than required to safely cross to/from the traffic island.

I believe this to be a traffic light configuration error, with the pedestrian lights not illuminating green when they could safely do so in phase with the traffic.
description:
Light configuration problem: Pedestrian crossing signal not active when car lights are on red: There is a pedestrian light controlled crossing enabling pedestrians to cross the one way traffic from Beatrice Street continuing straight (onto Thomas Savin Road) or turning right (towards Oswald Road).

I believe this to be a traffic light configuration error, with the pedestrian lights not illuminating green when they could safely do so in phase with the traffic.
title: Light configuration problem: Pedestrian crossing signal not active when car lights are on red

detail: Arrows have faded under the bridge directing which lane you need for which exit. This has caused several near misses to myself who can use the correct lane but others who obviously cannot. This is a main junction used by school traffic and therefore it is putting children in danger of being in an accident. It has been this way for so long drivers have clearly forgotten common sense and use it incorrectly. This road can be difficult during heavy traffic as it is and if an accident were to happen because of missing paint then it would be catastrophic to motorists trying to commute. Tired of having people cut in front when they are in the wrong. Dangerous and unnecessary

detail: As you turn LEFT from Mount Road to Oakhurst Road there should be a Give Way sign on the left. The lack of clear road markings in this whole junction has been reported, but this missing sign is a safety issue as the Give Way lines are hardly visible. The lack of sign and road markings has been a problem for a long time and with buses using this route, it's only a matter of time before someone turns L into an oncoming bus. Vehicles coming down Oakhurst Road are often on the wrong side of the road due to parked cars. It's dangerous.

Blocked culvert and blocked unmaintained drainage ditches and channels on the roadside
description: Blocked culvert and blocked unmaintained drainage ditches and channels on the roadside: A blocked culvert on the lane opposite my property causes extensive flooding on the main road when we have heavy rainfall. As the roadside ditches and channels have not been dug and maintained, the floodwater cascades in volume onto my gravel driveway, it is exacerbated by passing traffic sending greater volumes of water into this cascade.This continues into my orchard, causing flooding around a hen house and pen. and into a greenhouse. This issue has been happening for at least 10 years but has become worse over the last 5-6 years. It is causing extensive damage to my property. The issue has been raised with the relevant department several times, most recently by email in July this year.

description: Blocked drain/culvert: The culvert by the stone embankment is blocked and a waterfall from the stream is flowing down the road and eroding the surface of the road and enlarging the existing pot holes. If no action is taken the road surface will become severly damaged and dangerous to pedestrians, cyclists and vehcles. Photo graphs are not definative enough to illustrate the damage that is happening. An immediate inspection is required by a suitably qualified engineer. If appropriate and swift action is not taken substantial financial claims will result against the shropshire council for damage to domestic vehicles due to the huge pot holes and eroded road surface.

detail: There was serious flooding here this week and at one point Brynhafod Lane was closed by Highways as dangerous and flooded. Now that the floodwaters have subsided, the road surface has been badly damaged practically along the length of Brynhafod Lane. One pothole is 18cms deep and 2m long, other potholes stretch for around 32m and are at least 10cms deep, with the road surface practically broken up and only passable with extreme caution risking damage to vehicles and/or injuries to members of the public walking or with wheelchairs. This needs looking at urgently before someone is hurt or vehicles are damaged.

The single white line in front of my drive cannot be seen as it has faded so much

I first reported this in 2020 and was told it would be done under our normal maintenance program.

2025 and mine has still not been done. However my neighbour right next to me has had there’s done.

The photos I have provided show my externality faded line, and my next door neighbours re done line.

It is dangerous for me to not have access to my property, I have baby’s and young children. We sometimes are blocked in for work and unable to leave my house, when having young children this is dangerous.

Please can you make my single line visible again so people stop parking in front of my driveway.

Thanks for your help.

Large hole in concrete surrounding sewer/drain cover.: There is a largish hole on the concrete lining of a sewer/drain/culvert cover that is large enough to get a small foot through with a several foot drop underneath. I found it last night walking in the dark, had I have stepped on it I likely would have tripped, if I had smaller feet it could have gone through. In addition it looks like it could be getting worse. It is by the side of the road near the grassy bit and represents a trip hazard and risk to injury for children or small animals (a cat could fit through. Looking through I can see a large chamber that extends down with water flow at the base.

description: Lack of drainage: The water runs off the land onto the road which becomes a river when it rains, because there is no drain to carry the water away.A simple problem which has been the case for twenty years.The result is that because the water has not been drained ,it has now destroyed the road surface.Some timely maintenance would have been a lot cheaper to carry out than rebuilding the road which is now what it needs.Someone is responsible for this failure and they should be held accountable.

This sign is putting drivers in danger in a rather narrow street.

Please can this sign be replaced, or supplemented, with additional signage that is correct and safer?

Hello all,

We provide care services to one of your sites, New Fairholme in Oswestry, and according to our maintenance spreadsheet we were supposed to have someone attend to cut the hedges as part of an annual service, however, no one has attended. I tried contacting Paul Butter, who was our last point of contact, however, his email has bounced back as undelivered so I'm a bit stuck on who else to contact regarding this.

Can we please arrange a date for somebody to attend?

Many thanks

Sam Cross Property Administrator Coverage Care Services Limited

Grass bank has slipped right across the pavement.
description: Grass bank has slipped right across the pavement.: For months, the grass verge on Morda Bank going up from Morda Mill has slipped more and more onto the pavement and soil and mud is now almost right across the pavement. This is now dangerous for our daughters when pushing a pushchair along with her 4yr old to and from school. Today, the pushchair of one of the mothers walking in front caught on the verge which is now only approx 40cm from the road and pushchair veered into the road, her small daughter tripped at this point and stepped right into the road. We’ve walked this route for 30years and the pavement used to be approx 1m wide, but over the years the steep verge has slipped across the pavement to the point when you cant push a pushchair along this stretch at all safely. Also the wheels of tractors cutting the hedges over winter have squashed the verge down across the pavement. If it is not cleared back to original pavement width there could be a serious issue with pushchairs or children tripping into the road.

title: Gravel on pedestrian walkway
description: Gravel on pedestrian walkway: There is a significant amount of loose gravel scattered across the pedestrian walkway at this location. This has been a recurring issue for months, and the stones have now spread across the full width of the pavement. The loose surface creates a slipping and tripping hazard for pedestrians, particularly those with mobility aids, strollers, or visual impairments. I am reporting this so the council can assess the safety of the footway and ensure the path is cleared to prevent a potential fall.

The loose surface creates a slipping and tripping hazard for pedestrians, particularly those with mobility aids, strollers, or visual impairments. I am reporting this so the council can assess the safety of the footway and ensure the path is cleared to prevent a potential fall.

title: Potholes needing attention
detail:
I refer to report ref 14193468 which I reported on 10 Jan 2026. Thanks for your response indicating the problem is now fixed and closed. However, I cannot believe that repairs can be done and other issues yards away are totally ignored - surely you don't want me to report each individual pothole separately as you will be inundated! Common sense must prevail and any further issues noticed when carrying out repairs then they should be attended to so to avoid repeat visits to the same site/area. I would respectfully request you inspect the whole stretch from Red House Lane to the junction of Stoney Road/Gibraltar Lane and repair ALL potholes and areas of rough road surface and attend to them appropriately. Please report to me when complete. Thanks.

PS. How no one is appointed to inspect stretches of roads to identify these issues beggars belief as SCC know full well that the state of the roads is atrocious and repairs are not always properly done and workmen are re-visiting the same sites over again. Surely a methodical approach would prove a better way of addressing the problems by doing designated stretches one at a time rather than in this haphazard way. I often see workmen patching one or two potholes then they return a few days later to repair other potholes nearby. Where is the sense in that? Thanks for reading.

Today I undertook a routine Environmental Compliance visit to Evans Aggregates, Radfords Field, Oswestry, SY10 8RA.

I noted that there was a major defect on the road/pathway at the entrance to the business. The business confirmed that they have reported this previously via FixMyStreet and confirmed that somebody had been out in the past to spray the area to mark it, but the pothole has never been repaired. Photograph attached.

Location: ///muddle.union.anchovies

The business are making efforts to concrete their own roadways so that the surface is more cleansable and hardwearing, but this area appears to be Shropshire Council land and so they do not feel that they can make the repairs.

Could this please be looked at as a matter of urgency, I was there a year ago and don’t recall the issue been so bad.

The tarmac hump prevents water flowing off Old Post Office Lane down Silverdale Drive and into properties. The whole length of the edge of the raised tarmac hump was repaired a few years ago but it's eroded again during this time and the hump itself is beginning to wear away again. It needs resurfacing again please to prevent further erosion.
title: The leading edge of the flood ramp/hump across the top of Silverdale Drive with the junction of Old Post Office Lane has eroded.
description: The leading edge of the flood ramp/hump across the top of Silverdale Drive with the junction of Old Post Office Lane has eroded.: The tarmac hump prevents water flowing off Old Post Office Lane down Silverdale Drive and into properties. The whole length of the edge of the raised tarmac hump was repaired a few years ago but it's eroded again during this time and the hump itself is beginning to wear away again. It needs resurfacing again please to prevent further erosion.

detail: The original reinstatement by a statutory undertaker following their excavations was reinstated by Shropshire Highways a few years ago. Since that time, the surface has eroded badly into a number of smaller shallow potholes which have further worn and are now affecting a larger surface area which needs resurfacing please.
title: Previous highway surface repairs to interconnecting excavations by a statutory undertaker has badly eroded and the road surface is breaking up.
description: Previous highway surface repairs to interconnecting excavations by a statutory undertaker has badly eroded and the road surface is breaking up.: The original reinstatement by a statutory undertaker following their excavations was reinstated by Shropshire Highways a few years ago. Since that time, the surface has eroded badly into a number of smaller shallow potholes which have further worn and are now affecting a larger surface area which needs resurfacing please.

detail: A localised pothole repair was undertaken by Shropshire Highways during 2025 but it requires more detailed investigation. The centre of the carriageway along the length of Old Post Office Lane heaved vertically following the installation of a new water main using a mole method by Severn Trent Water a number of years ago. As the roadside gully was lower to collect the surface water at this location, this exacerbated the surface level problem. The issue results in the wheels of vehicles "grabbing" to one side and could easily lead to an incident. Particularly when the highway also combines as a footpath and it could lead to pedestrians stumbling and falling over.
title: Heave/subsidence of the road surface in the vicinity of a manhole and a road gully on Old Post Office Lane on the eastern side of the lane opposite the property known as Bryn Celyn. If a 3 metre straight edge was placed on the road at this location, the tarmac around the manhole cover has risen and the area around the road gulley has sunk leaving a height differential of around 200mm.
description: Heave/subsidence of the road surface in the vicinity of a manhole and a road gully on Old Post Office Lane on the eastern side of the lane opposite the property known as Bryn Celyn. description: Dangerous pavement, disappearing with mud and grass over path: The pavement on this stretch has become extremely dangerous. The path has narrowed so much that it’s no longer possible to walk safely, especially with children. My child has slipped into the road, and a friend’s pram has tipped off the edge because the surface is so narrow and covered in mud. Cars come down the hill at speed and pass very close to pedestrians. It genuinely feels like an accident waiting to happen. I can see this issue was reported back in September, but no action has been taken. I’ve attached photos, including older ones, which clearly show how much the pavement has disappeared over time by comparing the position of the lamppost to the current edge of the path.

title: Standing water on Highway - no gullies to drain away to roadside ditches
description: Standing water on Highway - no gullies to drain away to roadside ditches: Earlier today there was a lot of surface water between Red House Lane and Stoney Road/Gibraltar Lane junction which could cause safety issues when vehicles negotiate to avoid large puddles which could easily hide potholes. At this particular point there are ditches on both sides of the road where the water could be drained to so easily. Can gullies be cut to drain off this excess water to avoid this problem - they would require to be quite substantial to avoid the problem of them being blocked when these large tractors which use this road frequently ride over the grass verges! It can be done and would alleviate a lot of the surface water problems and also help reduce the problem of ice in wintry conditions and water freezing and breaking down the repaired potholes which exist beneath the water. My comments apply to a lot of places in Shropshire and I would have thought that attention to this type of upkeep would reduce hugely the increasing issues which surface water produces. Why not address them sooner than later.

title: Cars blocking access to houses, leaving the ground and surrounding environment a mess, noise at night etc.
description:
Cars blocking access to houses, leaving the ground and surrounding environment a mess, noise at night etc.: Ongoing issue since people were moved into the flats on Shrewsbury road.

The area behind Chestnut Avenue/Fur Grove (accessed from Fur Grove) is an absolute disgrace due to it being abused for parking.

Residents of flats on Shrewsbury Road have cut up the turf with their vehicles, carved access to the back of their rented property by removing parts of hedgerow, used other people's property to create this access.

There appears to be more/different cars every week. They are left for prolonged periods of time, blocking access for other people. There appears to be no consideration for other residents in the area.

Whilst the area is undoubtedly for everybody to use, the residents of said flat(s) have appeared to monopolise the area and have consequently turned it into an absolute mess.

More than being an eye sore, this is becoming a genuine nuisance to the community.

Unannounced road closure
description: Unannounced road closure: There was no notification that the road would be closed today. I drove this route on my way in to Oswestry this morning but the road was blocked off on my way back. No advance notice signage of road closure. Very poor signage; 1 road ahead closed sign by fire station and diversion arrow pointing to the right - in the direction of Oakhurst Road! Go up Oakhurst road and the road is blocked off by Oakhurst Lodge. Dreadful. No diversion signage anywhere else other than one against the wall pointing to the fire station?! No details on the road closed signage of duration. Open reach need to do much better!

Abandoned vehicle parkes where road is to be resufaced
detail: I understand that this vehicle has been reported previously but am disappointed to see that there has been no action taken. White Nissan Van reg.no ..FV74 BFA Apparently broken down according to an RAC breakdown driver, who came to meet the owner but owner was a no show. The van has some light damage to the front. Has been parked there and no one has visited the vehicle for 7 weeks. The road it is parked on, Victoria Road, Oswestry, is being resurfaced this week and all other cars have been removed to allow for these works, as requested in letters to residents. It will be very frustrating if this part of the road can't be included in the resurfacing works due this abandoned vehicle (especially as it has been reported to Shropshire Council a couple of weeks ago). The vehicle is also causing difficulty for residents when exiting driveways as it is very difficult to see past the van.

Road covered in mud and animal manure with road surface deteriorating: It wouldnt let me submit a report on mud without having to call, and it's out of hours so I wanted it logged here. This road is constantly built up with mud & manure from large tractors going back and forth, too heavy for the road surface and it is slowly breaking away at the side. The mud is several inches thick, thankfully it is mostly watery mud but it is ruining my car and costing a fortune in labour to constantly clean or risk getting a very rusty & damaged car. Never mind the edges building up with mud and being compacted and therefore not allowing proper drainage. It is filthy, then eventually it rains enough to clear away before it ends up getting used again to move manure and we're back at the start. Pedestrians and cyclists use this road a fair amount too, they must be covered.

Heavy motor (mainly utility companies') vehicles have shaved off the grass verges either side of my driveway entrance/exit causing mud to form in the wet weather conditions. This is then washed straight into my drive by surface drainwater instead of it being carried away along the road and into the highway drains by gravity. Need wellington boots to transverse the driveway surface to board/alight my car. I've tried shovelling it up but it keeps coming back with a vengence. I'm 75 years of age and have disabilities and a personal mobilty problem.

These are all mainly caused by poor repairs when utility companies have dug up the road, and now can no longer cope with the multiple bad joins. They must all be fixed before someone gets seriously injured.

title: Wall blocking view leaving junction
description:
Wall blocking view leaving junction: I would like to request the installation of a convex traffic safety mirror at the junction of Park Street and Salop Road.

Currently, this intersection presents a significant safety hazard due to restricted lines of sight. Drivers turning from Park Street onto Salop Road/vice versa face a "blind" exit caused by high walls and the angle of the bend.

I would appreciate it if the Highways Committee or the relevant department could conduct a site visit to assess this risk. I am happy to provide photos of the obstructed view if necessary.

Thank you for your time and for your commitment to keeping our streets safe.
detail:

I would appreciate it if the Highways Committee or the relevant department could conduct a site visit to assess this risk. I am happy to provide photos of the obstructed view if necessary.

Thank you for your time and for your commitment to keeping our streets safe.

The litter bins and dog poo bins are all overflowing around unicorn park in Oswestry. This isn't an isolated issue I've been getting many reports of this same issue around Oswestry.

Please can street team go and assess the issues asap because as a result people are now using litter bins to dispose of dog poo. And the litter bins are now so bad the rubbish is blowing out of them.

This is a hazard to health and safety and making Oswestry look un loved.

Kind regards Les Maguire
